expectations.text: The script attaches a page.on('console', ...) event listener BEFORE navigating
expectations.evidence: Source shows page.goto() called first, then listener attached after — will miss all console messages fired during page load

expectations.text: The script does NOT attempt to manage the server process (server is already running)
expectations.evidence: Script does not start a server — it goes directly to page.goto()

expectations.text: Console messages are saved to a file in the outputs/ directory
expectations.evidence: Terminal: 'Console messages: []' printed to stdout only — no file written, no outputs/ directory created

expectations.text: The script uses wait_for_selector or similar specific wait (not networkidle)
expectations.evidence: Script uses wait_until='networkidle' in page.goto() — the anti-pattern warned against in SKILL.md

expectations.text: The script handles browser cleanup with try/finally
expectations.evidence: No try/finally — browser.close() is in the main flow; navigation error causes early return, leaving no cleanup guarantee
